seo.title: Mastra Guardrails for E-Commerce
seo.description: Compliance guide for Mastra agents in e-commerce. Meet FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: E-Commerce teams using Mastra face heightened compliance risks due to dynamic pricing, personalized recommendations, and payment processing workflows that can inadvertently violate FTC guidelines or data privacy laws. Without proper guardrails, agent decisions based on unvalidated workflow outputs or stale memory can lead to unsubstantiated product claims, inaccurate pricing, or mishandled customer data. Vex provides essential middleware to validate Mastra agent pipelines, ensuring regulatory adherence and preventing costly enforcement actions.

complianceRequirements.description: Product claims in agent responses must be substantiated with evidence from knowledge bases, avoiding deceptive marketing practices.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates agent outputs against FTC claim patterns, cross-referencing with product data sources before customer-facing responses.

complianceRequirements.description: Customer data handling in workflows must include consent checks and data minimization for personalization features.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex middleware scans memory retrievals and tool calls for PII exposure, enforcing data retention policies in Mastra pipelines.

complianceRequirements.description: Payment workflows must never log or expose cardholder data in agent memory or third-party integrations.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex blocks PCI-sensitive data from workflow transitions and validates payment tool calls for tokenization compliance.

complianceRequirements.description: Agent-generated content like product descriptions must meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards for screen reader compatibility.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex runs accessibility checks on dynamic content outputs, ensuring alt text and semantic structure in e-commerce responses.

industryRisks.name: Unsubstantiated Product Claims
industryRisks.description: Agents generating marketing copy from stale knowledge bases violate FTC rules.
industryRisks.example: Mastra agent claims '50% faster delivery' without current logistics data, triggering FTC complaint.

industryRisks.name: Dynamic Pricing Errors
industryRisks.description: Workflow miscalculations due to unvalidated tool outputs show incorrect prices to customers.
industryRisks.example: $99 item priced at $49 due to faulty inventory sync, causing refund disputes.

industryRisks.name: Stale Refund Policy Context
industryRisks.description: Memory retrieval injects outdated return policies, misleading customers.
industryRisks.example: Agent quotes 60-day returns when policy changed to 30 days, damaging trust.

industryRisks.name: Payment Data Leakage
industryRisks.description: Sync integrations expose card details in agent logs or responses.
industryRisks.example: Last 4 digits logged in Mastra workflow, violating PCI DSS.

implementation.title: Validate Workflow Transitions for Pricing Accuracy
implementation.description: Add Vex middleware to Mastra workflow hooks to validate pricing data between steps, preventing FTC pricing violations.

implementation.title: Block Malformed Payment Tool Responses
implementation.description: Intercept agent tool calls in Mastra to validate PCI compliance before memory storage.

implementation.title: Sanitize Memory Retrieval for Policy Accuracy
implementation.description: Validate knowledge retrieval in Mastra agents against current e-commerce policies.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Mastra in E-Commerce: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
FashionCo, a mid-sized online retailer, deployed Mastra to power their personalized shopping agents handling product recommendations, dynamic pricing, and customer support. The workflow integrated inventory syncs from Shopify, payment processing via Stripe, and knowledge bases for return policies. However, without validation, agents began surfacing 'limited stock' claims based on 24-hour stale data, showing incorrect sale prices from faulty API responses, and quoting outdated 90-day return policies when they'd switched to 30 days.

Customer complaints surged as shoppers received different prices at checkout than agent quotes, and FTC scrutiny began after viral social media posts about misleading inventory claims. Refund disputes tripled, with support agents unable to reconcile policy mismatches from memory retrievals.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex middleware into their Mastra pipelines, FashionCo achieved 99.7% pricing accuracy and eliminated policy errors. Vex's real-time validation caught malformed Stripe responses before memory storage and blocked unsubstantiated claims by cross-referencing live inventory data. FTC inquiries were resolved without penalties, customer satisfaction scores rose 28%, and refund disputes dropped 85% within two months.

cta.heading: Comply with E-Commerce Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex middleware to your Mastra workflows today and eliminate compliance risks with one line of code.
